verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is canonically a mitochondrial matrix F1 catalytic subunit (beta), but substantial literature documents its ectopic display on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane of tumor cells, endothelial cells, and some immune cells (ecto-ATP synthase). This surface pool has been validated by surface biotinylation, flow cytometry on intact non-permeabilized cells, and functional antibody blocking studies. It serves as a receptor for angiostatin and apolipoprotein A-I on the cell surface. This qualifies as cell-state-induced / dual-localization surface accessibility in oncogenic and activated endothelial contexts, not baseline mitochondrial-only localization.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Mechanism of ecto-ATP synthase trafficking to PM outer leaflet remains unclear (no classical TM domain or GPI anchor); surface pool size and universality across cell types is variable.
